page.content: In a comparative analysis of sex hormones, the mean ± SD values were assessed for two groups: RMW n = 32 and PMW (n = 18), with a P value* column for statistical significance. The testosterone (total) levels were 1.15 ± 0.62 (0.7–2.8) nmol/L for RMW and 1.12 ± 0.57 (0.7–2.8) nmol/L for PMW, yielding a P value of 0.86. Progesterone levels were significantly higher in RMW at 4.0 ± 4.1 (0.6–4.7) nmol/L compared to PMW's 2.51 ± 1.95 (0.3–2.5) nmol/L, with a P value of 0.15.

18), with a P value* column for statistical significance. The testosterone (total) levels were 1.15 ± 0.62 (0.7–2.8) nmol/L for
RMW and 1.12 ± 0.57 (0.7–2.8) nmol/L for PMW, yielding a P value of 0.86. Progesterone levels were significantly higher in
RMW at 4.0 ± 4.1 (0.6–4.7) nmol/L compared to PMW's 2.51 ± 1.95 (0.3–2.5) nmol/L, with a P value of 0.15.

page.content: Estradiol levels showed RMW at 151 ± 161 (46–607) pmol/L versus PMW at 97.8 ± 66.0 (0–201) pmol/L, with a P value of 0.18. The FSH levels were markedly elevated in PMW at 77.6 ± 36.4 (26–135) IU/L compared to RMW's 6.15 ± 4.1 (3.5–12.5) IU/L, resulting in a P value of <0.0001. Similarly, LH levels were significantly higher in PMW at 40 ± 19.6 (8–59) IU/L relative to RMW's 3.71 ± 1.19 (2.4–12.6) IU/L, with a P value of <0.0001. In an examination of species development, the gestation period for Orangutans was 0.71 years, with a weaning age of 7.0 years, a total period of care of 7.7 years, and an age at first birth of 15.6 years, showing gestation/development at 4.4% and total care/development at 47.3%. Gorillas had a gestation of 0.70 years, weaning age of 2.8 years, total care of 3.5 years, first birth at 10 years, with percentages at 6.5% and 32.7%. Chimpanzees exhibited a gestation of 0.62 years, weaning at 4.5 years, total care at 5.1 years, and first birth at 13.3 years, with percentages at 4.5% and 38.6%. Bonobos had gestation at 0.65 years, weaning age of 4.5 years, total care of 5.1 years, first birth at 13 years, with percentages at 4.8% and 37.7%. Lastly, Humans had a gestation of 0.73 years, weaning age of 2.8 years, total care of 3.5 years, and an age at first birth of 19.5 years, with percentages at 3.6% and 17.4%.
